style_guide:
Extracted styles and formatting (from uploaded DOCX template)

Goal: Identify sections in the input text and in the template; align and style output according to the template. Use the exact style name as block_type. Numbering (1., 2., 3. or a., b., etc.) and bold phrasing are taken from the template.

Application rules (match template structure)

- Document title / main heading (court name, case caption, or document title like MOTION TO RESTORE) -> use style: Heading 1
- Section headers / subheadings (e.g. VERIFIED COMPLAINT, MEMORANDUM, FACTS, ARGUMENT, CONCLUSION, RELIEF REQUESTED, party captions) -> use style: Heading 6
- Body paragraphs (narrative, legal argument, facts, verification text, general content) -> use style: Normal
- Numbered or bulleted list items (allegations, motion grounds, relief items, any list where the template uses this style) -> use style: Normal (numbers 1., 2., 3. added automatically when applicable).
- Addresses (TO:, court or party address lines): use the same style as in the template (often Normal; one block per line if the template breaks them).
- Signature block (attorney name, firm, address, phone): use the template style for that block; use block_type signature_line for the underline line.
- Separator lines (dashes/dots ending in X) -> use block_type line and put the line characters in text.
- Signature underlines -> use block_type signature_line (optional label in text).
- Section underlines (solid line under a cause of action or heading) -> use block_type section_underline with empty text.
- Page break -> use block_type page_break with empty text before each new major section (as in the template).

Style definitions (font, alignment, indent, etc.)

Style: Normal

- 12.0pt
- space after: 0.0pt, left indent: 0.0pt, first line indent: 0.0pt, line spacing: 1.0pt

Style: Heading 1

- bold

Style: Heading 6

- italic, font: Cambria
- space before: 10.0pt

Style: Body Text Indent

- (default paragraph)

Style: Body Text

- (default paragraph)

Style: Body Text 2

- (default paragraph)

Style: Body Text 3

- (default paragraph)

Style: Body Text First Indent 2

- (default paragraph)

Style: Footer

- (default paragraph)

Style: Body Text Indent 2

- bold
- align: center, space after: 0.0pt, left indent: 0.0pt, line spacing: 1.0pt

Style: numbered indent paragraph

- line spacing: 1.0pt

Style: List Paragraph

- (default paragraph)

Style: Body Text Arial Single

- font: Times New Roman, 12.0pt
- space after: 0.0pt

Style: Body Text Titles

- font: Times New Roman, 12.0pt

Style: Balloon Text

- (default paragraph)

Style: Body Text First Indent

- (default paragraph)

Style: Body Text Indent 3

- 12.0pt
- left indent: 72.0pt

Style: No Spacing

- font: Times New Roman

Style: ec_msolistparagraph

- align: justify, space after: 0.0pt, left indent: 252.0pt

Style: annotation text

- (default paragraph)

Style: annotation subject

- (default paragraph)

Style: Default

- (default paragraph)

Style: Revision

- (default paragraph)

Per-section formatting (apply the following to the corresponding parts of the raw text):

• court_header: Use Style: Heading 1, bold. Example content: "SUPREME COURT OF THE STATE OF NEW YORK"
• court_header: Use Style: Normal. Example content: "COUNTY OF KINGS"
• body: Use Style: Normal. Example content: "--------------------------------------------------…"
• case_caption_index: Use Style: Normal. Example content: "MUMUNI AHMED,    	Index No.:"
• case_caption_parties: Use Style: Normal. Example content: "Plaintiff,"
• continuation: Use Style: Normal. Example content: "(empty line)"
• date_line: Use Style: Normal. Example content: "Dated:	New York, New York"
• continuation: Use Style: List Paragraph. Example content: "(empty line)"
• signature_line: Use Style: List Paragraph. Example content: "____________________________"
• body: Use Style: ec_msolistparagraph, alignment: justify. Example content: "MICHAEL COHAN, ESQ."
• case_caption_parties: Use Style: List Paragraph. Example content: "Attorneys for Plaintiff"
• body: Use Style: List Paragraph. Example content: "401 Park Avenue South, 10th Floor"
• recipients_header: Use Style: Normal. Example content: "TO:"
• body: Use Style: Body Text. Example content: "Plaintiff by his attorneys, COHAN LAW FIRM PLLC, a…"
• body: Use Style: Body Text Indent 2, alignment: center, bold. Example content: "AS AND FOR A FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION:"
• continuation: Use Style: Body Text. Example content: "(empty line)"

template_content.text: SUPREME COURT OF THE STATE OF NEW YORK
COUNTY OF KINGS
MUMUNI AHMED,    	Index No.:
Date Filed:
Plaintiff,
-against-
RICHARD MORALES,
Defendant.
TO THE ABOVE NAMED DEFENDANT:

You are hereby summoned to answer the complaint in this action, and to serve a copy of your answer, or if the complaint is not served with this summons, to serve a notice of appearance on the Plaintiff’s attorney(s) within twenty days after the service of this summons exclusive of the day of service, where service is made by delivery upon you personally within the state, or within 30 days after co...
The basis of venue is Plaintiff’s Residence: 8728 Flatbush Avenue, in Brooklyn, Kings County, New York.
Dated:	New York, New York
November____, 2022
MICHAEL COHAN, ESQ.
COHAN LAW FIRM PLLC
Attorneys for Plaintiff
401 Park Avenue South, 10th Floor
New York, New York 10016
TO:
RICHARD MORALES
85-49 79th Street
Woodhaven, New York 11421
SUPREME COURT OF THE STATE OF NEW YORK
COUNTY OF KINGS
MUMUNI AHMED,	Index No.:
Date Filed:
Plaintiff,
-against-
RICHARD MORALES,
Defendant.
Plaintiff by his attorneys, COHAN LAW FIRM PLLC, as and for a cause of action alleges, upon information and belief, as follows:
AS AND FOR A FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION:
NEGLIGENCE
At the time of the accident, Plaintiff, MUMUNI AHMED, was a lawful resident of County of Kings, State of New York.
That on August 11th, 2022, and at all times herein mentioned, Defendant, RICHARD MORALES, was a resident of the County of Queens, State of New York.
That on August 11th, 2022, and at all times herein mentioned, Defendant, RICHARD MORALES, was the owner of a 2021 Toyota motor vehicle bearing New York State license plate number KHS6947.
That on August 11th, 2022, and at all times herein mentioned, Defendant, RICHARD MORALES, was the registered owner of a 2021 Toyota motor vehicle bearing New York State license plate number KHS6947.
That on August 11th, 2022, and at all times herein mentioned, Defendant, RICHARD MORALES, operated a 2021 Toyota motor vehicle bearing New York State license plate number KHS6947.

That on August 11th, 2022, and at all times herein mentioned, Defendant, RICHARD MORALES, operated a 2021 Toyota motor vehicle bearing New York State license plate number KHS6947 on the roads of Kings County, City of Brooklyn and/or the road and public highways within the State of New York.
That on August 11th, 2022, and at all times herein mentioned, Defendant, RICHARD MORALES, controlled the use and operation of the aforesaid vehicle.
That on August 11th, 2022, and at all times herein mentioned, Defendant, RICHARD MORALES, maintained the aforesaid vehicle.
That on August 11th, 2022, and at all times herein mentioned, Defendant, RICHARD MORALES, inspected the aforesaid vehicle.
That on August 11th, 2022, and at all times herein mentioned, Defendant, RICHARD MORALES, supervised the use and operation of the aforesaid vehicle.
That on August 11th, 2022, and at all times herein mentioned, Defendant, RICHARD MORALES, repaired the aforesaid vehicle.
That on August 11th, 2022, and at all times herein mentioned, Defendant, RICHARD MORALES, managed the aforesaid vehicle.
That on August 11th, 2022, and at all times herein mentioned, Defendant, RICHARD MORALES, was the lessee of a 2021 Toyota motor vehicle bearing New York State license plate number KHS6947.
That on August 11th, 2022, and at all times herein mentioned, Defendant, RICHARD MORALES, was the lessor of a 2021 Toyota motor vehicle bearing New York State license plate number KHS6947.
That on August 11th, 2022, and at all times herein mentioned, Plaintiff, MUMUNI AHMED, was a lawful bicyclist of an e-scooter.

That on August 11th, 2022, and at all times herein mentioned, 4th Avenue at or near its intersection with 19th Street, County of Kings, City of Brooklyn and State of New York, (hereinafter, “the location”) was and still is a public thoroughfare.

That on August 11th, 2022, at the aforesaid location, the 2021 Toyota motor vehicle bearing New York State license plate number KHS6947 owned and operated by Defendant, RICHARD MORALES, came in contact with the plaintiff, MUMUNI AHMED, a lawful bicyclist of an e-scooter.
That as a result of the foregoing, the Plaintiff, MUMUNI AHMED, was injured.
That as a result of the foregoing, the Plaintiff, MUMUNI AHMED, was seriously and permanently injured.

That the aforesaid contact and accident and the injuries resulting therefrom, were due solely and wholly as a result of the careless, reckless, and negligent manner in which the Defendant owned, operated and controlled the motor vehicle without the Plaintiff in any way contributing thereto.

The above-stated occurrence and the result thereof was caused by the negligence of the Defendant in the ownership, operation, management, maintenance, inspection, repair and control of said motor vehicle; in operating same without due regard to the rights and safety of the Plaintiff; in operating said motor vehicle in a manner which unreasonably endangered the Plaintiff; in failing to obey traffic...
That the aforesaid occurrence and its results thereof were in no way due to any negligence or culpable conduct on the part of the Plaintiff.
That this action falls within one or more of the exception set forth in CPLR 1601 and CPLR 1602.

Pursuant to CPLR § 1602(2)(iv), Defendant is jointly and severally liable for all of Plaintiff’s damages, including but not limited to Plaintiff’s non-economic losses, irrespective of the provisions of CPLR § 1601, by reason of the fact Defendant owed the Plaintiff a non-delegable duty of care.

Plaintiff's damages, including, but not limited to Plaintiff's non-economic losses, irrespective of the provisions of CPLR § 1601, by reason of the fact that each of the answering Defendant is vicariously liable for the negligent acts and omissions of his agents, servants, or employees, which persons or entities may be one or more of the co-Defendant to this action.

Pursuant to CPLR § 1602(5), Defendant is jointly and severally liable for all of Plaintiff’s damages, including but not limited to Plaintiff's non-economic losses, irrespective of the provisions of CPLR § 1601, by reason of the fact that Defendant wrongful conduct was intentional.

Pursuant to CPLR § 1602(7), Defendant is jointly and severally liable for all of Plaintiff's damages, including but not limited to Plaintiff’s non-economic losses, irrespective of the provisions of CPLR § 1601 by reason of the fact that Defendant acted with reckless disregard for the safety of others.

Pursuant to CPLR § 1601(11), Defendant is jointly and severally liable for all of Plaintiff’s damages, including but not limited to Plaintiff’s non-economic losses, irrespective of the provisions of CPLR § 1601, by reason of the fact Defendant acted knowingly or intentionally, and in concert, to cause the acts or failures which are a proximate cause of Plaintiff’s injuries.

That by reason of the negligence of the Defendant as aforementioned, MUMUNI AHMED, sustained serious bodily injuries, causing him great pain and discomfort in the past, present, and upon information and belief, in the future; he became and continues to be sick, sore, lame and disabled; upon information and belief, some of his injuries will be permanent; he was and upon information and belief, will...

By reason of the wrongful, negligent and unlawful actions of the Defendant, as aforesaid, the Plaintiff, MUMUNI AHMED, sustained serious injuries as defined in §5102(d) of the Insurance Law of the State of New York, and has sustained economic loss greater than basic economic loss as defined in said Insurance Law.

By reason of the foregoing and the negligence of said Defendant, the Plaintiff, MUMUNI AHMED is informed and verily believes, his aforesaid injuries are permanent and that he will permanently suffer from the effects of the aforesaid injuries and that he will be caused to suffer pain, suffering, significant limitations and consequential limitations of use of the affected body parts; and disruption ...

By reason of the foregoing, the Plaintiff, MUMUNI AHMED, was compelled and did necessarily require medical aid and attention and did necessarily pay and become liable therefore, for medicines and upon information and belief, the Plaintiff, MUMUNI AHMED, will necessarily incur additional similar expenses.
By reason of the foregoing, the Plaintiff, MUMUNI AHMED, has been unable to attend his usual occupation and vocation in the manner required.
That by reason of the foregoing, the Plaintiff has been damaged in sums, which exceeds the jurisdictional limits of all lower courts, which would otherwise have jurisdiction.
Dated:	New York, New York
November_____, 2022
Yours, etc.;
MICHAEL COHAN, ESQ.
COHAN LAW FIRM PLLC
Attorneys for Plaintiff
401 Park Avenue South, 10th Floor
New York, New York 10016

template_structure.text: SUPREME COURT OF THE STATE OF NEW YORK

template_structure.hint: You are hereby summoned to answer the complaint in this action, and to serve a c…
template_structure.text: You are hereby summoned to answer the complaint in this action, and to serve a copy of your answer, or if the complaint is not served with this summons, to serve a notice of appearance on the Plaintiff’s attorney(s) within twenty days after the service of this summons exclusive of the day of service, where service is made by delivery upon you personally within the state, or within 30 days after completion of service where service is made in any other manner. In case of your failure to appear or answer, judgment will be taken against you by default for the relief demanded in the complaint.

template_structure.hint: The above-stated occurrence and the result thereof was caused by the negligence …
template_structure.text: The above-stated occurrence and the result thereof was caused by the negligence of the Defendant in the ownership, operation, management, maintenance, inspection, repair and control of said motor vehicle; in operating same without due regard to the rights and safety of the Plaintiff; in operating said motor vehicle in a manner which unreasonably endangered the Plaintiff; in failing to obey traffic signals; in failing to properly steer, guide, manage and control said vehicle, so as to prevent same from striking the plaintiff, a lawful bicyclist of an e-scooter; in operating same at a rate of speed greater than was reasonable and proper at the time and place of the occurrence; in failing to apply the brakes or slow down or stop in such a manner as would have prevented the occurrence; in failing to have made adequate and timely observation of and response to conditions then existing at the place and time of the occurrence; in failing to observe signs and signals prevailing at the time and place of the occurrence; in failing to keep a proper look-out when operating and controlling said vehicle; in failing to properly maintain said motor vehicle; in failing to give adequate and timely signal notice or warning of the approach of his vehicle; in striking the plaintiff, a lawful bicyclist of an e-scooter; in operating said motor vehicle in violation of the applicable vehicle and traffic laws, rules, regulations, statutes and ordinances of Kings County and State of New York.
